headline: High School Student Creates AI Tool to Diagnose Autism and ADHD
meta_description: Edward Kang's AI tool, RetinaMind, diagnoses autism and ADHD from retinal images with 89% accuracy, developed while still in high school.
summary_short: A high school student developed RetinaMind, an AI system that diagnoses autism and ADHD from retinal images with 89% accuracy.
body:
Edward Kang, a high school student, has developed an innovative artificial intelligence tool called RetinaMind that analyzes retinal images to diagnose neurodevelopmental disorders with remarkable precision. The system achieves an 89% diagnostic accuracy rate for both autism and ADHD—conditions that can be challenging and time-consuming to identify through traditional clinical methods.

RetinaMind represents a significant breakthrough in making neurodevelopmental diagnostics faster and more accessible. By using retinal imaging, a relatively simple and non-invasive procedure, the tool could help identify autism and ADHD in people who might otherwise go undiagnosed, particularly in underserved communities.

Kang's achievement demonstrates the potential of young innovators to tackle real-world medical challenges. Early and accurate diagnosis of these conditions can open doors to earlier intervention and support. His work suggests that AI-powered diagnostic tools may soon play an important role in making neurodevelopmental care more equitable and efficient.
